---
title: "Session 8: Your AI Strategy"
subtitle: "Business Intelligence & AI"
author: "Kerry Back, Rice University"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, revealjs-style.scss]
    slide-number: c
    transition: fade
    navigation-mode: linear
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.05
    center: true
---

## The Course Journey {.shrink}

::: {.timeline}
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
1
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 1
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
Exploring Data with AI --- first conversation with your data
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
2
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 2
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
Our Data Is Everywhere --- the cross-system challenge
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
3
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 3
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
How It Works Under the Hood --- agents, SQL, and prompts
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
4
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 4
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
From Data to Deliverable --- reporting pipelines
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
5
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 5
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
What Does Deployment Look Like --- from prototype to production
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
6
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 6
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
Can You Trust It --- validation, governance, guardrails
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
7
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Session 7
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
Document AI --- answers from your own files
:::
:::
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-dot}
8
:::
::: {.timeline-label}
Today
:::
::: {.timeline-desc}
Your AI Strategy --- capstone and synthesis
:::
:::
:::

---

## Today's Objective {.centered-statement}

## Present a credible, scoped AI adoption strategy and leave with a concrete next step

---

## The Setup {.section-divider}

---

## Your Audience {.centered-statement}

## You are presenting to your executive team. Your breakout group is your board of advisors.

::: {.explainer}
Convince them this is worth funding.
:::

---

## Capstone Format {.shrink}

::: {.step-flow}
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
1
:::
::: {.step-title}
Breakout Pitches
:::
::: {.step-desc}
5 groups of 6. Each person presents for 3 minutes + 2 min feedback. Group votes on the strongest.
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
2
:::
::: {.step-title}
Plenary Presentations
:::
::: {.step-desc}
5 winning strategies presented to the full class. 5 min each + 3 min Q&A from peers.
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
3
:::
::: {.step-title}
Instructor Feedback
:::
::: {.step-desc}
Feedback on pilot scope, business case, governance, and Day 1 action for each strategy.
:::
:::
:::

---

## What Makes a Strong Pitch {.shrink}

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
Evaluation Dimensions
:::

- **Specificity** --- a concrete use case, not "we'll use AI for analytics"
- **Business case** --- who benefits, what decision gets faster, what's the dollar impact
- **Data readiness** --- where does the data live, what's the integration challenge
- **Governance** --- who validates answers, what are the guardrails
- **Day 1 action** --- what happens Monday morning, not "we'll form a committee"
:::

::: {.explainer}
The best pitches are [narrow and actionable]{.accent}. "We'll deploy document AI on our 200 vendor contracts so procurement can answer compliance questions in seconds" beats "We'll implement an enterprise AI strategy."
:::

---

## Breakout Round {.section-divider}

---

## Breakout Instructions {.shrink}

::: {.two-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Your Pitch (3 minutes)
:::

- What's the use case?
- What data or documents does it use?
- Who are the users and what decision does it improve?
- What's your Day 1 action?
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Your Feedback (1 minute)
:::

- Is the scope tight enough to pilot in 30 days?
- What's the biggest risk?
- What question would the CFO ask?
- Vote: which pitch should represent the group?
:::
:::

---

## Plenary Presentations {.section-divider}

---

## Synthesis: What You Now Know {.shrink}

::: {.four-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Plain English = Data Answers
:::

AI agents turn natural language questions into SQL queries, chart generation, and narrative summaries --- no technical skills required.
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Simple but Not Easy
:::

The technology is simpler than expected (50 lines of code) but production deployment requires governance, validation, and organizational change.
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Two Complementary Tools
:::

Database AI for structured data (numbers, trends, KPIs). Document AI for unstructured text (policies, contracts, procedures). Most real questions need both.
:::

::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
The Critical Skill
:::

The human skill that matters most: asking the right questions and verifying the answers. AI amplifies judgment --- it doesn't replace it.
:::
:::

---

## Beyond Data {.section-divider}

---

## AI as Thinking Partner {.centered-statement}

## The real power isn't asking what happened. It's asking [what should we do]{.accent} --- and pushing back on the answer.

::: {.explainer}
AI anchors on whatever framing you provide. The human role is challenging assumptions, not outsourcing judgment.
:::

---

## The Thinking Partner Demo {.shrink}

::: {.two-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
Data Question
:::

"What were Q4 sales for the Energy Division?"

- One answer. One number.
- Useful, but limited.
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
Strategic Question
:::

"I'm considering expanding the Energy Division. What are the top 3 risks based on our data?"

- Synthesizes across sales, headcount, customer concentration, margin trends
- Then: "Push back on risk #2 --- what's the evidence?"
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
The shift from "report pulling" to "strategic dialogue" is where the real ROI lives. But remember: AI anchors on your framing. [Challenge its assumptions]{.accent} the way you'd challenge a consultant.
:::

---

## Monday Morning Actions {.shrink}

::: {.four-cards}
::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
A. Audit Workflows
:::

Where do people wait for data? Which recurring reports follow the data → analysis → deliverable pattern?
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
B. Scope a Sprint
:::

Pick one team, one data source, 30 days. Build a prototype and test with real questions from real users.
:::

::: {.card .card-light}
::: {.card-title}
C. Inventory Documents
:::

Where is knowledge trapped? Policies, contracts, procedures --- which collections would benefit from AI-powered Q&A?
:::

::: {.card .card-dark}
::: {.card-title}
D. Establish Governance
:::

Who validates AI output? Who approves deployment? Define this before shadow IT does it for you.
:::
:::

::: {.explainer}
You don't need a 12-month roadmap. You need [one pilot, one owner, one deadline]{.accent}.
:::

---

## The 30-Day Sprint {.shrink}

::: {.step-flow}
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
Week 1
:::
::: {.step-title}
Pick & Prep
:::
::: {.step-desc}
Choose one use case. Identify the data source. Get stakeholder buy-in.
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
Week 2
:::
::: {.step-title}
Build & Test
:::
::: {.step-desc}
Connect the data. Build the prototype. Test with real questions from real users.
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
Week 3
:::
::: {.step-title}
Validate
:::
::: {.step-desc}
Compare AI answers to known results. Document accuracy. Identify failure modes.
:::
:::
::: {.step-card}
::: {.step-icon}
Week 4
:::
::: {.step-title}
Decide
:::
::: {.step-desc}
Go/no-go for expanded pilot. Present findings. Define Phase 2 scope.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Resources {.shrink}

::: {.info-box}
::: {.box-title}
Continue Your Learning
:::

- **Meridian Corp environment** --- remains available for 90 days after the course
- **Course materials** --- all slides, case studies, and demo scripts on the course site
- **Recommended reading** --- curated list of papers, vendor evaluations, and case studies
- **Office hours** --- monthly drop-in sessions for the next quarter
- **Peer network** --- your breakout group is your first call when you hit a wall
:::

---

## Final Takeaway {.section-divider}

---

## The companies that win with AI {.centered-statement}

## won't have the best technology. They'll have the best [questions, answers, and governance]{.accent}.

::: {.explainer}
Now go build something.
:::
